title: AI Agents for Beginners
bestFor: People who want practical AI agent workflows without pretending every tool is magic.
roomRead: Closest practical San Antonio option. Good for workflow notes, safety questions, and beginner-friendly language.
questionToAsk: What is one real task an agent can safely handle this week without handing it the keys to everything?
followUpNote: Write down one workflow, one guardrail, and one phrase normal people would actually understand.
captionSeed: AI agents are more useful when the room talks about work, limits, and examples instead of hype.

title: AI Meetup - Scale AI Workflows
venue: Coder Austin, 520 E Oltorf St, Austin
bestFor: Developers and platform teams moving AI workflows from experiments into production.
roomRead: Worth the Austin drive if the agenda gets specific about reliability, deployment, evaluation, or team process.
questionToAsk: Where do AI workflows break first when they move from a demo to a real team?
followUpNote: Capture one scaling lesson, one tool name, and one concrete failure mode to research later.
captionSeed: The interesting AI conversations start when the demo is over and the workflow has to survive real users.

title: Austin Robotics & AI
bestFor: Robotics, embodied AI, and people who like their machine learning attached to the physical world.
roomRead: Higher technical ceiling. Best when you want a smarter-than-average room and a topic with good explainer potential.
questionToAsk: What does AI need to understand about the physical world that text-only systems miss?
followUpNote: Look for one analogy that makes robotics AI easier to explain to non-engineers.
captionSeed: Robotics makes AI feel less like a chat box and more like a negotiation with the real world.

title: ACE June Meetup: AI for Reach and Distribution
bestFor: Creatives and entrepreneurs using AI for audience growth, SEO, newsletters, and social content.
roomRead: Good fit when the goal is creator strategy, not just technical depth. Watch for practical examples over generic growth talk.
questionToAsk: What AI-assisted distribution habit is actually sustainable for a small creator or solo business?
followUpNote: Turn the best answer into a creator checklist, then note whether it fits TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, or a blog.
captionSeed: AI distribution advice only counts if a busy person can repeat it next week.

title: AI & Machine Learning Startups Networking
bestFor: Founders, investors, pitch practice, and casual market-signal gathering.
roomRead: Useful if you want names, patterns, and founder pain points. Less useful if you need a structured technical workshop.
questionToAsk: What AI problem are people willing to pay for right now, not just talk about?
followUpNote: Log three startup angles, one overused claim, and one person worth following up with.
captionSeed: Startup networking is field research when you listen for what people are actually trying to buy.
